title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Overactive Bladder
intro: Herbal teas for Overactive bladder are a natural remedy that involves drinking tea made from specific herbs to alleviate symptoms associated with this condition, which is characterized by a sudden urge to urinate frequently, often accompanied by involuntary loss of urine. These herbal teas can be effective in treating overactive bladder due to their ability to relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce inflammation, allowing for better bladder control. Some examples of herbal teas that have been used to treat overactive bladder include chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the bladder; peppermint tea, which can help reduce spasms in the bladder and improve digestion; saw palmetto tea, which may help reduce the frequency of urination; and uva ursi tea, which has antibacterial properties that can help prevent urinary tract infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as dandelion root tea, juniper berries tea, and marshmallow root tea have also been used to treat overactive bladder due to their ability to reduce inflammation, improve bladder function, and promote overall urinary health.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as stinging nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder (OAB) due to their relaxing and calming effects on the urinary system. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat OAB by reducing bladder muscle spasms, decreasing the frequency of urination, and alleviating urgency. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and hormones involved in bladder function. By consuming Urtica dioica teas, individuals with OAB may experience benefits such as improved bladder control, reduced symptoms of urinary incontinence, and enhanced quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it to remove the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Stir well to combine.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 1 hour before meals, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be an effective treatment for overactive bladder, but they may also cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and diarrhea in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount as needed, and to avoid taking the tea in large quantities or for extended periods, as this may lead to dependency and worsen symptoms.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Urtica dioica tea for overactive bladder
Start with 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 1 week
Gradually increase to 1-2 cups, 3-4 times a day, as needed
Use tea for 3-6 months, or as recommended by your doctor
Monitor your symptoms and adjust dosage or frequency under doctor's guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder ailments due to their calming and relaxing properties, which help to soothe the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of urgency and frequency. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as valerenic acid, valeranone, and valepotriates, which have been shown to exhibit anti-spasmodic and anxiolytic effects that help to reduce muscle contractions in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, Valeriana officinalis teas help to improve bladder control and reduce the frequency of urination, providing relief from the symptoms of overactive bladder. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat overactive bladder include improved quality of life, reduced symptoms of urgency and frequency, and a natural and non-invasive treatment option that can be used in conjunction with other therapies.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 cup of cold water and pour it into a teapot.
Add the dried Valeriana officinalis root to the cold water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root.
Drink the Valeriana officinalis tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset due to the sedative properties of valerenic acid. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Valeriana officinalis teas in large quantities or for extended periods, as this may lead to increased sedation and interactions with other medications, particularly those used to treat depression and anxiety.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 30 minutes before bedtime
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water
Gradually increase dose to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed
Use 2-3 times a day for 6-8 weeks to see noticeable results
Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosing and guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder, a condition characterized by an uncontrollable urge to urinate. The properties of this herbal preparation, such as its calming and relaxing effects, help to reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and alleviate symptoms associated with overactive bladder. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been found to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscles, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. By consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced symptoms of overactive bladder, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the frequency of bathroom visits.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active ingredients.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the flowers.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking.
Drink 1-2 cups of Lavandula angustifolia tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, headaches, and interactions with certain medications that can worsen the condition. Precautions when using these teas include starting with low doses, avoiding use in children and pregnant women, and monitoring blood pressure, as high levels of lavender can cause blood pressure to drop.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Take for 4-6 weeks to notice improvements in overactive bladder symptoms.
Consult a doctor or healthcare professional for guidance and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat the overactive bladder ailment due to their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the bladder and reduce symptoms of urgency and frequency. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus, including flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and allowing for more efficient bladder emptying. The anti-inflammatory properties of Astragalus membranaceus teas also help to reduce inflammation in the bladder and surrounding tissues, further alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Astragalus membranaceus teas may provide relief from overactive bladder symptoms, improving quality of life for those affected by this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1-2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and place it in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Astragalus membranaceus root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the Astragalus membranaceus root. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of Astragalus membranaceus tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, fatigue, and changes in blood pressure, which can be exacerbated in individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions. Precautions should be taken when using Astragalus membranaceus teas, including drinking them in moderation, avoiding interactions with other medications, and monitoring blood sugar levels, especially in individuals with diabetes.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Astragalus membranaceus tea daily to reduce bladder symptoms.
Start with a dosage of 2 tablespoons of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably before meals and bedtime.
Continue using the tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable improvements in bladder control.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger teas, have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder (OAB) symptoms by relaxing the bladder muscles and reducing the frequency of urination. The bioactive constituents of ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe the bladder and alleviate symptoms of OAB. These compounds also have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the bladder, reducing the urge to urinate and improving bladder control. Regular consumption of ginger tea has been shown to provide relief from OAB symptoms, including nocturia, urinary frequency, and urgency, making it a beneficial herbal preparation for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey into a separate container (optional).
Add the dried Zingiber officinale root to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root. Add honey if desired.
Drink the tea slowly and regularly (2-3 cups a day) to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a potential treatment for overactive bladder, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, and also interact with certain medications, like blood thinners. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it to minimize the risk of side effects, and also to avoid consuming it before surgery or with other medications that may increase its blood-thinning effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with a small dosage of 1/2 teaspoon of dried ginger per cup.
Gradually increase dosage to 1 teaspoon if needed, as directed by a doctor.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for at least 4-6 weeks to see noticeable effects.
Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea if you have kidney or bladder issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas, also known as horsetail tea, have been used for centuries to treat overactive bladder, a condition characterized by sudden and uncontrollable urges to urinate. The diuretic and astringent properties of Equisetum arvense teas help to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination, providing relief from this ailment. The bioactive constituents of Equisetum arvense teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the urinary tract. By consuming Equisetum arvense teas, individuals can benefit from a reduction in symptoms associated with overactive bladder, including incontinence, frequency, and nocturia.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Equisetum arvense (Horsetail roots) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried Equisetum arvense in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be effective in treating overactive bladder, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, such as stomach upset and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin irritation and itching. Precautions to take when using Equisetum arvense teas to treat this ailment include monitoring your body's response, avoiding consumption if you experience severe side effects, and being cautious when using it in combination with other medications or herbal supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Equisetum arvense tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250ml, adjust to 500ml if needed, after 2 weeks.
Take tea for 2-3 months for noticeable improvements.
Combine with healthy diet and regular exercise for best results.
Monitor and adjust tea usage as needed to manage symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Crataegus monogyna teas, also known as hawthorn tea, have been traditionally used to treat an overactive bladder due to its antispasmodic and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and improve bladder control. These properties help to treat the overactive bladder by reducing muscle spasms, increasing urine storage capacity, and promoting a normal bladder function. Regular consumption of Crataegus monogyna teas may provide relief from symptoms such as urinary incontinence and frequent urination, offering a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Crataegus monogyna leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, while also being mindful of interactions with other medications and potential allergic reactions to the tea's constituents.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Crataegus monogyna tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons if needed and symptoms persist.
Take tea 30 minutes before meals to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
Continue using Crataegus monogyna tea for at least 2 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder (OAB) due to their diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins in Cassia auriculata teas help to relax the bladder muscle, reduce inflammation, and prevent oxidative stress, thereby alleviating OAB symptoms. The tea helps to improve bladder control by regulating bladder contractions and reducing the frequency of urination, providing relief from OAB symptoms such as urgency, frequency, and nocturia. The benefits of Cassia auriculata teas in treating OAB include improved bladder function, reduced symptoms, and enhanced quality of life, making it a promising herbal remedy for managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Collect 1 cup of Cassia auriculata flowers and leaves. You can find them in health food stores or online.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of Cassia auriculata flowers and leaves to the boiling water.
Steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to treat overactive bladder symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches in some individuals, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications, and it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed to minimize the risk of side effects like stomach upset and allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat the overactive bladder ailment, and its properties of being anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic help to alleviate the symptoms associated with this condition. The herbal preparation helps to treat overactive bladder by relaxing the muscles in the bladder and urethra, thereby reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been found to have a soothing effect on the bladder muscles and may also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. By using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, individuals with overactive bladder can experience benefits such as reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and a decrease in the need for medications or surgical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils. Reduce heat to a simmer.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the simmering water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage symptoms of overactive bladder.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may have antioxidant properties that could be beneficial in the treatment of overactive bladder, given its high radical-scavenging activity comparable to other medicinal herbs.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but possible side effects may include high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and edema due to its diuretic properties. Precautions should be taken by monitoring blood pressure and potassium levels, avoiding excessive consumption, and being aware that individuals with heart or kidney conditions may be more susceptible to these side effects.

The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat OAB include reduced symptoms, improved bladder control, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with this condition.

Take 1/2 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder. You can buy this at a health food store or online.
Add the powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Then, remove from heat.

preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, also known as artichoke tea, have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder ailments due to their antispasmodic and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents present in artichoke tea, including cynarin and saponins, help to relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce the frequency of urination. By inhibiting the contraction of bladder muscles, artichoke tea helps to alleviate the symptoms of overactive bladder, such as incontinence and urgency. The benefits of artichoke tea in treating overactive bladder include improved bladder control, reduced symptoms, and enhanced quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cynara scolymus (artichoke) leaves.
Use 1/4 cup of boiling water to make the tea. Pour the boiling water over the artichoke leaves in a cup.
Steep the artichoke leaves for 5-7 minutes. This will help the tea's active ingredients to release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the artichoke leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. Precautions should be taken when using Cynara scolymus teas, including avoiding consumption if you have kidney or liver problems, and not drinking it in large quantities or for extended periods to prevent potential interactions with other medications and exacerbation of underlying conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including the overactive bladder ailment. The antispasmodic and astringent properties of this herbal preparation help to calm muscle spasms and reduce urinary incontinence. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects, such as reducing muscle contractions and relaxing the bladder muscles. By using Hypericum perforatum teas, individuals can benefit from its ability to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, including frequent urination and incontinence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort) flowers and leaves.
Use a tea infuser or a strainer to measure 2-3 teaspoons of the dried herb into a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, 30 minutes before meals, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used as an alternative treatment for overactive bladder, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of hypericin. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as starting with small doses, avoiding concurrent use with other medications, and monitoring blood pressure, as excessive consumption may lead to serotonin syndrome and interact with certain medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder (OAB) due to their relaxing and calming properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms of this condition. The herbal preparation's ability to act as a urinary tract relaxant, improving bladder control and reducing the frequency of urination, makes it an effective treatment option. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, particularly bilobalide and ginkgolides, play a crucial role in treating OAB by modulating the autonomic nervous system and reducing inflammation in the bladder. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms of OAB, improved bladder health, and enhanced overall quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Heat the water in a pot over medium heat until it boils.
Add the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then remove from heat.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves. Drink the tea immediately.
preparations.preparation_study: According to American Journal of Health-System Pharmacy: AJHP: official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists, Ginkgo biloba teas have been studied for circulatory disturbances and dementia, but there is no specific mention of their use for overactive bladder.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can potentially cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset when used to treat overactive bladder, especially when consumed in large amounts or combined with other medications. To safely use Ginkgo biloba teas for this purpose, take regular breaks from consumption to allow your body to recover, monitor your blood pressure and adjust your dosage accordingly, and avoid using it with other substances that may interact with its active compounds, such as blood thinners and certain antidepressants.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder (OAB) symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The benefits of using milk thistle teas to treat OAB include reduced symptoms of urgency and frequency, improved bladder control, and a decrease in the risk of urinary tract infections.

Strain the tea into a cup and discard the fennel seeds. You can add honey or lemon to taste.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Catha edulis teas can be used as a treatment option for overactive bladder, but possible side effects may include increased heart rate, anxiety, and insomnia due to the stimulant properties of cathinone, a compound found in the plant. Precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, as it may exacerbate the condition or interact with other medications, and it's recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas have been traditionally used to treat the overactive bladder ailment due to their antispasmodic and anticholinergic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce the frequency of urination. The bioactive constituents of Rauvolfia serpentina, such as reserpine and ajmaline, work together to inhibit the release of neurotransmitters that stimulate the bladder muscles, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing the urgency and frequency of urination, improving the quality of life for individuals suffering from overactive bladder. The benefits of using Rauvolfia serpentina teas to treat overactive bladder include reduced symptoms, improved bladder control, and a decrease in the risk of urinary incontinence.
preparation_procedure: Get 1/2 cup of dried Rauvolfia serpentina root. You can buy it from a health store or online.
Put 2 tablespoons of the dried root into a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Remove the pot from the heat and add the tea infuser or muslin bag with the Rauvolfia serpentina root.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser or muslin bag and drink the tea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rauvolfia serpentina teas can be used to treat overactive bladder symptoms, but possible side effects include dizziness, drowsiness, and changes in blood pressure. Precautions when using these teas include starting with small amounts and gradually increasing dosage, avoiding consumption before driving or operating heavy machinery, and monitoring blood pressure levels to prevent potential complications.

preparations.herb_desc: Ruscus aculeatus teas have been used traditionally to treat overactive bladder ailment, and research suggests that it may be effective in reducing symptoms. This herbal preparation contains flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce bladder muscle spasms and improve bladder function. The bioactive constituents of Ruscus aculeatus teas, including aucubin and caffeic acid, may help to relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urination. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the bladder muscles, Ruscus aculeatus teas may provide a natural and effective treatment option for overactive bladder, improving quality of life for those affected by this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ruscus aculeatus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help manage overactive bladder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ruscus aculeatus teas can be used to treat overactive bladder, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its potential interaction with other medications or exacerbating pre-existing conditions like kidney problems or heart issues. Precautions to take when using Ruscus aculeatus teas include avoiding its use with other diuretics, monitoring kidney function, and starting with a low dose to assess tolerance, as excessive consumption may worsen symptoms or lead to dehydration.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat overactive bladder, a condition characterized by involuntary urine leakage and urgency. The properties of Melissa officinalis teas that help to treat this ailment include their antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and anticholinergic effects, which help to relax the bladder muscles and reduce bladder spasms. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and geraniol, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they can help to reduce bladder contractions and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat overactive bladder include improved bladder control, reduced frequency of urination, and decreased risk of urinary tract infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Remove the pot from heat and add the dried Melissa officinalis leaves.
Steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat the overactive bladder ailment, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Melissa officinalis teas to treat this ailment, it is essential to be cautious and consider the potential interactions with other medications, as well as to monitor your body's reaction and adjust the dosage accordingly to avoid adverse effects.

intro_study: According to the study, teas for overactive bladder are being researched and show promise as an alternative herbal treatment, although further studies are needed to confirm their efficacy and safety.
